daily_brief: The day's most consequential developments center on the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing, where both leaders claimed progress on trade while remaining divided on Taiwan and Iran [Reuters via Globe and Mail], [Nikkei Asia], [RFI]. Markets reacted nervously, with Boeing shares falling on underwhelming deal announcements [FT] and Asian tech stocks sliding amid rising oil prices linked to the ongoing Iran conflict [Nikkei Asia]. Russia launched its largest aerial assault on Ukraine since the war began, killing at least 27 civilians over two days and demolishing residential buildings in Kyiv—actions President Zelensky said contradict Russian claims the war is ending [BBC], [Globe and Mail], [RFI]. In a dramatic political collapse, Latvia's Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned after controversy over Ukrainian drones that strayed into Latvian territory and struck an oil facility, with NATO analysts suggesting the drones may have autonomously selected their target [Globe and Mail], [BBC]. Humanitarian concerns mounted as the UN appealed to Equatorial Guinea not to deport US asylum seekers to countries where they face persecution [Guardian], while a federal judge ordered the Trump administration to return a Colombian woman mistakenly deported to the Democratic Republic of Congo [Guardian].

top_stories.headline: Trump and Xi Conclude Beijing Summit with Trade Optimism but Deep Divisions on Taiwan and Iran
bullets: US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping met over two days in Beijing, the first visit by a US president to China in nine years
Trump claimed 'fantastic trade deals' were reached covering agriculture, aviation, and AI, while Xi described a new 'constructive strategic and stable relationship'
Xi warned that mishandling Taiwan could lead to 'a clash' between the two nations; Trump said China offered to 'help on Hormuz' regarding the Iran conflict
Boeing shares fell after the summit as investors deemed the announced deals disappointing; progress was reported on fentanyl precursor trafficking

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized that leaders 'talked past each other' on key issues despite warm rhetoric

sources.name: Nikkei Asia
sources.framing_note: Focused on business delegation including Musk, Cook, and Huang; detailed Chinese foreign ministry statements

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Led with Trump's 'fantastic deals' claim; included Xi's promise to send rose seeds to White House

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Focused on Boeing share decline and market disappointment with deal outcomes

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasized 'new common understandings' from Chinese foreign ministry perspective

top_stories.headline: Russia Launches Largest Aerial Assault on Ukraine Since War Began, Killing Dozens
bullets: Russia launched over 1,560 drones and 56 missiles over a two-day period targeting Ukrainian cities, the largest such attack since the 2022 invasion began
At least 27 civilians were killed, including two children, with a Kyiv apartment building demolished and over 50 residential buildings damaged nationwide
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 93% of projectiles, downing 652 drones and 41 missiles overnight
President Zelensky said the attacks contradict Russian claims the war is ending; Friday declared a day of mourning in Kyiv

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Detailed casualty figures and rescue operations; quoted Zelensky on partners needing to hold Moscow accountable

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Contrasted Putin's Saturday statement that war was 'coming to an end' with the scale of attacks

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Emphasized that attacks 'shredded hopes' of a halt to Moscow's invasion

top_stories.headline: Latvian Government Collapses After Ukrainian Drones Strike Oil Facility; AI Targeting Suspected
bullets: Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned after her coalition collapsed following controversy over two Ukrainian drones that struck an oil depot in eastern Latvia on May 7
NATO Strategic Communications Centre director said drones likely made an 'autonomous decision' to select the target after losing contact with operators—potentially a first in warfare
No air-raid sirens sounded until after both drones had struck; Defense Minister Andris Spruds was fired over the incident before his party withdrew support from the government
Ukraine officially claims the drones were redirected by Russian electronic warfare; NATO analysis contradicts this account

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Led with AI targeting angle; quoted NATO think tank director extensively on autonomous decision-making

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Focused on political crisis and coalition collapse; less emphasis on AI targeting theory

top_stories.headline: UN Urges Equatorial Guinea to Halt Deportations as Judge Orders Return of Colombian Woman from DRC
bullets: A federal judge ruled the deportation of Colombian asylum seeker Adriana María Quiroz Zapata to the Democratic Republic of Congo was 'likely illegal' and ordered her return to the US
UN human rights experts issued a rare public appeal urging Equatorial Guinea not to return US deportees to their home countries where they face persecution, torture, or death
The Trump administration paid Equatorial Guinea $7.5 million to accept third-country nationals who had asylum protections in the US
Quiroz Zapata, 55, has diabetes and thyroid conditions; she reported skin deterioration and blackened nails while in detention and now lives under supervision in a Kinshasa hotel

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Detailed individual deportee's medical condition and hotel confinement; described Equatorial Guinea as 'one of the world's most repressive regimes'

top_stories.headline: Ebola Outbreak Declared in Eastern DR Congo with 65 Deaths Reported
bullets: Africa CDC declared an Ebola outbreak in DR Congo's Ituri province, with 246 cases and 65 deaths reported, mainly in gold-mining towns of Mongwalu and Rwampara
This is the 17th Ebola outbreak in DR Congo, where the disease was first discovered in 1976
Laboratory tests at the national research institute confirmed the virus in 13 of 20 samples analyzed
Africa CDC is convening meetings with Uganda, South Sudan, and international partners on cross-border surveillance; additional suspected cases reported in provincial capital Bunia

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Straightforward reporting on outbreak declaration and case numbers; noted Congolese government had not yet officially declared outbreak

CIA Director John Ratcliffe meets Cuban intelligence officials in Havana amid deepening oil crisis from US blockade

US Supreme Court temporarily restores mail access to abortion pill mifepristone while litigation continues
Japanese memory chip maker Kioxia forecasts 48-fold quarterly profit increase driven by AI data center demand
Global conflict-driven internal displacements reached record 32.3 million in 2025, surpassing disaster-driven displacement for first time
Millions of Palestinians worldwide mark 78th anniversary of the Nakba amid ongoing displacement in Gaza
UK political turmoil deepens as Andy Burnham announces challenge to Prime Minister Keir Starmer, sending gilts and sterling lower
Five Italian divers, including University of Genoa professor, die in cave diving accident in Maldives at 50-meter depth
FIFA announces Madonna, Shakira, and BTS to headline first-ever World Cup final halftime show at MetLife Stadium
